ogTitle: Atkinsons Dam vs Aubigny: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Atkinsons Dam and Aubigny.
heading: Atkinsons Dam vs Aubigny — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Atkinsons Dam, Queensland and Aubigny, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Aubigny has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Atkinsons Dam (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Atkinsons Dam or Aubigny safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Atkinsons Dam has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Aubigny scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Atkinsons Dam scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Aubigny scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Atkinsons Dam or Aubigny?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Aubigny has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
